# testdata/ — M0 validation harness input

This folder is empty until the maintainer populates it (~90 min job, see
PLAN_v2.md "Testdata construction standard"). Nothing here is fabricated or
synthetic — every item must be a real, currently-active eBay listing.

## Folder layout

```
testdata/
  <item-folder-name>/
    item_id.txt      # required — one eBay item ID, from the listing URL
    expected.json     # generated by `python -m pipeline.groundtruth testdata/`
    notes.md          # optional — seller_context free-text for this item
    photos/
      *.jpg / *.png / *.heic
```

- `<item-folder-name>` can be anything descriptive (e.g. `01-levis-501-mens`,
  `12-vintage-no-tag-dress`) — the folder name has no meaning to the code
  beyond being a unique identifier in reports.
- `photos/` must be the **original files as shot** (phone camera roll / laptop
  folder), not eBay's recompressed copies — and must include ruler/tag/flaw
  shots even if they were never uploaded to the live eBay listing.

## item_id.txt

One eBay item ID, taken from the listing URL (the numeric ID after
`/itm/...`, or eBay's RESTful `v1|...|0` form). Whitespace is stripped.

## expected.json schema

Generated by `python -m pipeline.groundtruth testdata/` via the Browse API
`getItem` call — never hand-typed for the fetched fields:

```json
{
  "itemId": "v1|123456789012|0",
  "title": "eBay's live listing title",
  "categoryId": "11483",
  "categoryPath": "Clothing, Shoes & Accessories|Men|Men's Clothing|Jeans",
  "aspects": { "Brand": "Levi's", "Size": "34x32", "...": "..." }
}
```

Plus optional keys the maintainer may add **by hand** — the validator treats
these as optional, and `groundtruth` preserves them across regeneration
(it never overwrites them):

```json
{
  "measurements": { "pit_to_pit": "21 in", "length": "30 in" },
  "package": { "weight_oz": 18.5, "l": 12, "w": 9, "h": 3 },
  "known_flaws": ["small stain on left cuff"]
}
```

**Sanity pass required before trusting the diff:** skim the live listing for
wrong specifics — it was built with the workflow this project replaces, and
its errors are now baked into "ground truth." Hand-correct known-wrong values
directly in `expected.json` and note the correction in `notes.md`.

## notes.md (optional)

Free-text seller context for this item — same content that would go in the
app's one-line intake note (brand if tagless, provenance, known flaws, etc).
If present, `python -m pipeline.validate testdata/` runs the item **twice**
— with and without seller_context — and reports both, so the value of her
notes on tagless/vintage items is measurable.

## Stratification target (10–15 items total)

An all-easy set makes the accuracy report lie. Aim for:

- 3–4 clearly branded items with tag photos
- 3–4 vintage/no-tag or blurry-tag items
- 2 shoes
- 1–2 accessories
- ≥2 items with ruler measurement photos
- 1 item with a scale/notebook weight shot, if any exist
- 1–2 items with described flaws
- 1 patterned/multi-color item

## Running the harness

```
python -m pipeline.groundtruth testdata/   # fetch/refresh expected.json per item
python -m pipeline.validate testdata/      # run the pipeline, score against expected.json
```
